steps.title: Find the cause of avoidance
steps.content: The passage directly connects Mrs. Ochiltree's “frankness at times no less than startling” comments about people to her consequence: she is “more or less avoided.” This causal link is the key signal.

steps.title: Apply the cause to the choices
steps.content: Frank, startling remarks about people would naturally offend them, explaining why they avoid her. The other three either reverse the dynamic, invent unsupported details, or misattribute the cause. That confirms D is correct.
